diagrams.title: Tabula Recta (Vigenère Square)
diagrams.description: A 26x26 table used for Vigenère encryption/decryption. Each row represents a Caesar cipher with shift equal to the row letter.
elements: Shows example: Row K + Column A = K
diagrams.purpose: Visual reference for performing Vigenère encryption and decryption by finding intersections of key and plaintext letters.

diagrams.title: Vigenère Encryption Process
diagrams.description: Diagram showing how the key is applied cyclically to encrypt plaintext.
elements: Mapping shows each plaintext letter + corresponding key letter = ciphertext
Position 1: A + C = C, Position 2: T + I = B, etc.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the repeating application of the key to encrypt longer messages, demonstrating the periodic nature of Vigenère.

diagrams.title: Frequency Analysis Comparison
diagrams.description: Bar chart comparing letter frequencies in: (a) English plaintext, (b) Vigenère ciphertext with short key, (c) Vigenère ciphertext with long key, (d) Random text
elements: (b) Some flattening but still visible peaks
(c) Nearly flat distribution similar to random
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how increasing key length in polyalphabetic ciphers progressively 'smears' the frequency distribution, hiding patterns from attackers.
